data: 6 generators (A-F), load options 400/600/700 MW; 3 scales: 2x2 (4q), 3x2 (6q), 4x2 (8q); same as baseline
method: QAOA (p=2 layers, 5 restarts, COBYLA optimizer) with cqlib StatevectorSimulator
optimal_rate: 2/3 (67%) exact optimum at 4q and 6q; 2.16% gap at 8q
qubo_modeling: H = sum x[i,t]*C_i + lambda*(D_t - sum x[i,t]*P_i)^2, QUBO normalized to [-1,1]
limitations: Simulator-only results (cqlib StatevectorSimulator), NOT real quantum hardware. QAOA is heuristic: finds exact optimum at small scales (4-6 qubits) but shows a 2.16% optimality gap at 8 qubits — this gap reflects QAOA's known limitation on larger combinatorial problems and would benefit from more layers/restarts. Optimality measured against brute-force (exact for n<=8). All solutions are feasible (load-balance satisfied).
